title: Olearia praetermissa
taxonomy: Olearia praetermissa is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the aster or sunflower family. The genus Olearia is a part of the larger group, comprising shrubs and small trees, primarily native to Australia and New Zealand. The species name praetermissa refers to the fact that it was previously overlooked or missed in classification. Through further research, its unique characteristics have allowed for its distinction and recognition within the Olearia genus.

intro: Olearia praetermissa, also known as New Zealand tree daisy or tree olearia, is a shrub native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its small white flowers and woody stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health and immune function. Bioactive constituents of Olearia praetermissa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Olearia praetermissa is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
